5:["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["path",{"d":"m9 18 6-6-6-6","key":"mthhwq"}]],"className":"lucide-chevron-right text-stone-300 shrink-0","size":13}]

7:["$","li","2",{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":"$5:props:iconNode","className":"lucide-chevron-right text-stone-300 shrink-0","size":13}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/blog?category=Gear","className":"hover:text-orange-500 transition-colors","children":"Gear"}]]}]
8:["$","li","3",{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":"$5:props:iconNode","className":"lucide-chevron-right text-stone-300 shrink-0","size":13}],["$","span",null,{"className":"text-stone-600 font-medium line-clamp-1","children":"Best Desk Setup for Productivity in 2026: The Complete Workstation Guide"}]]}]
9:["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-white dark:bg-stone-900 rounded-2xl p-6 sm:p-8 mt-4 shadow-lg border border-stone-100 dark:border-stone-800 mb-10","children":[["$","h1",null,{"className":"text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl font-bold text-stone-900 dark:text-stone-100 leading-tight mb-5","style":{"fontFamily":"'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif"},"children":"Best Desk Setup for Productivity in 2026: The Complete Workstation Guide"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"flex flex-wrap items-center gap-4 text-sm text-stone-400","children":[["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/authors/james-park","className":"flex items-center gap-2 group","children":[["$","img",null,{"src":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1507003211169-0a1dd7228f2d?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","alt":"James Park","className":"w-8 h-8 rounded-full object-cover object-top"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-200 text-sm group-hover:text-orange-600 transition-colors","children":"James Park"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-400","children":"Tech Reviewer"}]]}]]}],["$","span",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"12","cy":"12","r":"10","key":"1mglay"}],["path",{"d":"M12 6v6l4 2","key":"mmk7yg"}]],"className":"lucide-clock","size":13}]," ","13 min read"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"March 28, 2026"}]]}]]}]
a:["$","div",null,{"className":"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10 pb-16","children":[["$","article",null,{"className":"lg:col-span-2","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-stone-600 leading-relaxed mb-10 font-medium pl-4 border-l-4 border-orange-500","children":"Monitor selection, keyboards, mice, cable management, accessories — everything you need to build a desk setup that makes work faster and less draining. Gear picks for every budget."}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"the-setup-that-makes-work-inevitable","children":"The Setup That Makes Work Inevitable"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The best desk setup is not the most impressive-looking one. It is the one that makes starting work frictionless, staying focused easy, and ending the day clean. This guide covers every element — monitors, keyboard, mouse, lighting, cable management, and the accessories most people overlook — with specific gear recommendations at every budget level."}}],["$","h2","2",{"id":"monitor-your-most-important-hardware-decision","children":"Monitor: Your Most Important Hardware Decision"}],["$","p","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Your monitor choice affects posture, eye strain, screen real estate, and how long you can work before fatigue sets in. This is not the place to cut costs."}}],["$","h3","4",{"id":"single-vs-dual-monitor","children":"Single vs. Dual Monitor"}],["$","p","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A single large monitor (32–34 inches) is often better than two smaller ones. The mental cost of context-switching between two screens is higher than most people realise. A single ultrawide delivers comparable real estate without the visual break down the centre of your workflow."}}],["$","p","6",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Dual monitors make sense when you genuinely need to reference one document while working in another: financial analysis, video editing, coding with documentation open. For most knowledge workers, a single large display is the cleaner choice."}}],["$","h3","7",{"id":"monitor-specs-that-actually-matter","children":"Monitor Specs That Actually Matter"}],["$","ul","8",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Resolution</strong>: Minimum 1440p (2560×1440) for 27 inches. 4K is ideal for 32 inches and above."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Panel type</strong>: IPS for accurate colours and wide viewing angles. OLED for the best contrast and colour accuracy but at a significant premium."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Refresh rate</strong>: 60 Hz is sufficient for office work. 120 Hz makes scrolling feel smoother and reduces eye strain over long sessions."}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>USB-C with Power Delivery</strong>: Powers your laptop and carries the display signal over a single cable — the biggest single cable management improvement you can make."}}]]}],["$","h3","9",{"id":"recommended-monitors","children":"Recommended Monitors"}],["$","p","10",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>LG 27UN880 (27-inch 4K, ~£450)</strong>: IPS panel with USB-C 96W power delivery and an integrated Ergo arm. One cable between laptop and monitor."}}],["$","p","11",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Dell UltraSharp U3423WE (34-inch Ultrawide, ~£650)</strong>: IPS Black panel with excellent contrast, USB-C, built-in KVM switch for two computers. The best option for multi-device users."}}],["$","p","12",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>LG 32UN880 (32-inch 4K, ~£600)</strong>: The larger sibling of the 27UN880, with the same Ergo stand. Ideal for those who primarily work from a fixed desk."}}],"$Ld","$Le","$Lf","$L10","$L11","$L12","$L13","$L14","$L15","$L16","$L17","$L18","$L19","$L1a","$L1b","$L1c","$L1d","$L1e","$L1f","$L20","$L21","$L22","$L23","$L24","$L25","$L26","$L27","$L28"]}],"$L29","$L2a","$L2b"]}],"$L2c"]}]
b:["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js","async":true}]
c:["$","$L2d",null,{"children":["$","$2e",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@2f"}]}]
d:["$","h2","13",{"id":"keyboard-the-case-for-mechanical","children":"Keyboard: The Case for Mechanical"}]
e:["$","p","14",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The keyboard is your primary input device. A poor keyboard — mushy keys, poor travel, unstable base — adds low-level friction to every word you type. Over the course of a workday, this accumulates."}}]
f:["$","p","15",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Mechanical keyboards use individual switches under each key rather than a rubber membrane. The result: more consistent actuation, significantly longer lifespan (50–100 million keystrokes vs. 5–10 million for membrane), and feedback that reduces errors."}}]
10:["$","h3","16",{"id":"switch-types-for-productivity","children":"Switch Types for Productivity"}]
11:["$","ul","17",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Tactile (Brown, Topre)</strong>: A bump when the key actuates without an audible click. Ideal for mixed-use environments."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Linear (Red, Speed)</strong>: Smooth keystroke with no bump. Preferred by fast typists. Very quiet."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Clicky (Blue, Green)</strong>: An audible click at actuation. Satisfying but loud — avoid in shared spaces."}}]]}]
12:["$","h3","18",{"id":"form-factors","children":"Form Factors"}]
13:["$","ul","19",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Full size</strong>: Includes number pad. Takes up significant desk width."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>TKL (Tenkeyless)</strong>: Removes the number pad, bringing the mouse approximately 4 inches closer — a meaningful ergonomic benefit."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>65%</strong>: Compact, includes arrow keys, no function row. Best for small desks and travel."}}]]}]
14:["$","p","20",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Recommended keyboards</strong>: Keychron K2 (65%, ~£75), Logitech MX Keys (wireless, excellent for offices, ~£100), NuPhy Air75 (wireless, low profile, ~£95). For a full comparison of switch types and layouts, see our <a href=\"/blog/best-mechanical-keyboards-2025\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">mechanical keyboards guide</a>."}}]
15:["$","h2","21",{"id":"mouse-precision-and-wrist-health","children":"Mouse: Precision and Wrist Health"}]
16:["$","p","22",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Your mouse hand is under sustained mechanical stress for hours at a time. A mouse requiring significant grip force, awkward wrist angles, or excessive movement accelerates fatigue and increases the risk of repetitive strain."}}]
17:["$","h3","23",{"id":"mouse-options","children":"Mouse Options"}]
18:["$","p","24",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Ergonomic (shaped)</strong>: Designed to fit the right hand naturally. More comfortable than a symmetric mouse for most users. The Logitech MX Master 3S (~£95) is the best mainstream option — excellent precision, customisable buttons, and a horizontal scroll wheel that transforms spreadsheet and timeline work."}}]
19:["$","p","25",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Vertical</strong>: Holds the hand in a handshake position (thumb up) — the natural resting angle of the wrist. Significantly reduces pronation strain. The Logitech MX Vertical (~£90) is the best option in this category."}}]
1a:["$","p","26",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Trackball</strong>: The ball moves, not the device. Excellent for users with shoulder or elbow strain — the arm barely moves at all. The Logitech MX ERGO (~£85) is the standard. For a full breakdown of ergonomic mouse types, see our <a href=\"/blog/best-ergonomic-mice-2025\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">ergonomic mice guide</a>."}}]
1b:["$","h2","27",{"id":"desk-accessories-worth-buying","children":"Desk Accessories Worth Buying"}]
1c:["$","p","28",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Monitor arm</strong>: Frees the entire desk surface by eliminating the monitor stand. Allows perfect positioning. Ergotron LX (~£120) is the standard recommendation — it handles most monitors up to 34 inches and lasts indefinitely."}}]
1d:["$","p","29",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Desk pad</strong>: A large leather or felt pad protects the surface, unifies the visual aesthetic, and provides a consistent mouse surface. Underrated quality-of-life improvement for under £30."}}]
1e:["$","p","30",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>USB-C hub</strong>: If your laptop has limited ports, a Thunderbolt 4 hub connects your monitor, keyboard, mouse, external storage, and ethernet through a single cable. CalDigit TS4 (~£280) is the best if budget allows; Anker 777 (~£80) is the best mid-range option."}}]
1f:["$","p","31",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Under-desk cable tray</strong>: Moves the power strip and cable clutter out of sight without permanent installation. Around £15–25, pays off immediately."}}]
20:["$","p","32",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Laptop stand</strong>: If you use a laptop with an external monitor, a stand positions the laptop vertically to save desk space and improve ventilation."}}]
21:["$","h2","33",{"id":"the-clean-desk-protocol","children":"The Clean-Desk Protocol"}]
22:["$","p","34",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The best desk setup is maintained, not just designed. A useful protocol:"}}]
23:["$","ul","35",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Every morning</strong>: Clear anything that does not belong. One cup, nothing else, on the surface."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Every Friday</strong>: File loose papers, coil and cable-tie any loose cables, wipe the desk surface."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Every quarter</strong>: Audit accessories. If something has not been used in three months, it does not earn desk space."}}]]}]
24:["$","p","36",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A clear desk is not an aesthetic preference — it is a cognitive one. Visual clutter increases cortisol and reduces the ability to focus on a single task."}}]
25:["$","h2","37",{"id":"the-minimum-viable-setup-under-400","children":"The Minimum Viable Setup (Under £400)"}]
26:["$","p","38",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Starting from scratch on a limited budget:"}}]
27:["$","ol","39",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Monitor</strong>: LG 27MK430H (27-inch 1080p) — acceptable quality for the price (~£150)"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Keyboard</strong>: Keychron K2 with Brown switches (~£75)"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Mouse</strong>: Logitech MX Anywhere 3 (~£55)"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Monitor arm</strong>: Ergotron LX (~£120)"}}]]}]
28:["$","p","40",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Total: approximately £400. You will feel the difference from a basic setup on day one."}}]
29:["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-10 pt-8 border-t border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm font-medium ","Gear"]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-4","children":[["$","$L3","best-ergonomic-office-chairs-2026",{"href":"/blog/best-ergonomic-office-chairs-2026","className":"flex gap-3
